mechanism_check: The implementation of the key mechanism is severely incomplete. Only Stage-1 (Multi-Cue Coarse Gate) is partially implemented. C1 (variance) and C3 (Sobel-edge max) are present. C2 (entropy) is implemented but uses a highly inefficient CPU-bound NumPy loop. C4 (Mahalanobis) is just a placeholder. Stage-0 (Reference Prep), Stage-2a/b (Encoder, AdaIN, FAISS spatial search), and Stage-3 (EVT, Selective Blending) are completely missing.
spec_alignment: The design only partially addresses the Stage-1 portion of the spec. The overall Pipeline Architecture, GPU-accelerated micro-batching, and memory bank logic are absent from the provided snippet.
metric_evaluation_path: Not visible. There is no evaluation logic, EVT thresholding, or metric calculation present in the provided snippet to track FPR, AUROC, or Inference Time.
constraints_compliance: The latency constraint (<0.5 ms/patch and <60s overall) is directly violated by moving tensors to CPU and using NumPy inside a loop for entropy calculation. The rest of the constraints cannot be evaluated due to missing code.

issues.description: Stage-1 C2 (Entropy) computes histogram on CPU using NumPy in a loop (`patches[i, 0].cpu().numpy()`). This causes catastrophic CPU-GPU sync latency and violates the <0.5 ms/patch GPU-acceleration constraint.
issues.design_suggestion: Vectorize the entropy calculation entirely on GPU. Scale the patch to [0, 255], cast to integer, and use `torch.bincount` or `torch.histc` in a batched manner without leaving the GPU.

issues.description: Stage-1 C4 (Mahalanobis micro-screen) is hardcoded to zeros.
issues.design_suggestion: Integrate Block-1 of the frozen encoder into the MultiCueCoarseGate or pass the pre-computed Block-1 features to it, and compute the actual Mahalanobis distance using stored Stage-0 reference statistics.

issues.description: Stage-2a, Stage-2b, and Stage-3 are entirely missing from the model architecture.
issues.design_suggestion: Implement the FrozenEncoder with robust AdaIN (median/MAD) at blocks 1-2, deep feature extraction, FAISS k-NN coreset with ±r=4 spatial search, and EVT (GPD) thresholding layers.

issues.description: The MultiCueCoarseGate assumes `acceptance_percentile` is a single scalar but the hypothesis dictates independent acceptance bands (1st-percentile calibrated) for different cues.
issues.design_suggestion: Update `reference_stats` to hold explicit threshold dictionaries (e.g., min/max bounds) for variance, entropy, edge_max, and mahalanobis derived from Stage-0.

missing_components: Stage-0 Robust Reference Preparation (Median/MAD profiling, greedy k-center coreset)
Stage-2a Frozen Encoder (DINOv2/EfficientNet) with Shallow-Layer AdaIN
Stage-2b Dense Spatial Scoring with FAISS and ±r=4 spatial search window
Stage-3 Selective Assembly, Gaussian Blending, and Two-Level EVT (GPD) Thresholding
Spectral Validation Protocol (Fourier high-frequency cosine similarity and FDR checking)
unnecessary_complexity: Using CPU-bound NumPy histograms for 8-bit Shannon entropy instead of native PyTorch operations.
bottlenecks: The `.cpu().numpy()` call inside the patch processing loop will completely bottleneck the async micro-batching pipeline.
overall_comment: The current snippet only scratches the surface of the Stage-1 Coarse Gate and contains a critical performance flaw in the entropy calculation. The author must replace the CPU loop with vectorised GPU ops, flesh out the Mahalanobis cue, and implement the rest of the missing pipeline stages (Encoder with AdaIN, FAISS spatial search, EVT) to fulfil the hypothesis contract.
